commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor affected-path proof plus maintainer acceptance of the compatibility cap, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff reduces an unbounded provider-response DoS risk and does not add dependencies, workflows, secret handling, or new code execution surfaces. (sha=5500d1543508a6e83a6addb035fc6d916a1c6968)
commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces Bedrock Mantle and HuggingFace successful model-discovery `response.json()` reads with `readProviderJsonResponse` and updates Mantle discovery tests to use real `Response` objects.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +12. Total +20 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main calls `response.json()` on successful Mantle and HuggingFace catalog responses, and the shared reader tests/proof show why that read is load-bearing. I did not run an affected-path oversized streaming repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor affected-path proof plus maintainer acceptance of the compatibility cap, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(agents): bound model catalog JSON response reads for bedrock-mantle and huggingface
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Amazon Bedrock Mantle model discovery (`/v1/models`) and HuggingFace model
discovery (`/models`) both parse successful HTTP responses with an unbounded
`await response.json()`. On Node, `response.json()` buffers the entire body
before parsing, so a provider-controlled endpoint can stream an arbitrarily
large or never-ending JSON payload and push the discovery path into memory
pressure, hangs, or OOM.

That matters here because both inputs are untrusted:

- Mantle discovery reads from an operator-selected AWS endpoint.
- HuggingFace discovery reads from an external router endpoint.

This PR closes the same response-body gap already fixed for sibling catalog
surfaces in `#95418`, `#95420`, and `#96027`, but for the two remaining bundled
provider discovery reads still using unbounded success-path JSON parsing on
current `main`.

## Changes

- Replace the Mantle success-path read in `discoverMantleModels` with
  `readProviderJsonResponse(response, "bedrock-mantle.model-discovery")`.
- Replace the HuggingFace success-path read in
  `discoverHuggingfaceModels` with
  `readProviderJsonResponse(response, "huggingface.model-discovery")`.
- Reuse the existing shared provider JSON reader instead of introducing a new
  provider-local helper. That helper already enforces the standard 16 MiB JSON
  byte cap and wraps malformed JSON with a caller label.
- Preserve the existing fail-soft behavior around the changed call sites:
  Mantle still falls back to cached models or `[]` on discovery failure, and
  HuggingFace still falls back to the static bundled catalog on discovery
  failure.
- Update Mantle discovery tests to return real `Response` objects instead of
  plain `{ json() {} }` mocks so the changed path uses the same body/stream API
  shape as production.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** a successful catalog response with no
  `Content-Length` must not be buffered whole if it exceeds the JSON safety cap;
  the read must stop near the cap, cancel the stream, and raise a bounded
  overflow error instead of draining the full body.
- **Real environment tested:** a real local `node:http` server bound to
  `127.0.0.1`, streaming a JSON body in 64 KiB chunks with **no**
  `Content-Length`, driven through the real exported bounded reader
  `readResponseWithLimit` that `readProviderJsonResponse` uses internally.
- **Exact proof shape:**
  1. Stream an oversized JSON body (~24 MiB) from `/huge`.
  2. Drive `readResponseWithLimit(response, 1 MiB, { onOverflow })` against the
     stream and assert bounded rejection plus early socket close.
  3. Run the negative control `await response.json()` against the same `/huge`
     stream and measure full-body drain.
  4. Drive the same bounded helper against `/small` and confirm a normal
     catalog-shaped JSON body still parses intact.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Bounded case: threw
    `bedrock-mantle.model-discovery: JSON response exceeds 1048576 bytes`.
  - Early cancel: the server socket closed after only **1,064,178 bytes**
    reached the wire, near the 1 MiB cap rather than the full ~24 MiB body.
  - Negative control: the old unbounded `response.json()` path pulled the full
    **25,165,824 bytes**, proving the cap is load-bearing.
  - Happy path: a small valid catalog body still parsed into exactly one model
    with its `id` intact.
- **Suite coverage:** the in-repo Vitest run passed **5/5 files, 48/48 tests**,
  including the updated Mantle discovery tests that now use real `Response`
  objects on the success path.
- **What this proof does and does not prove:** this demonstrates real bytes on
  wire, early stream cancellation, and unchanged small-body parsing through the
  exact bounded transport helper the new call sites use. It does **not** yet
  directly drive `discoverMantleModels` or `discoverHuggingfaceModels` against
  a hostile local streaming endpoint.

## Evidence

```text
[case 1] oversized catalog JSON, cap=1024 KiB, NO content-length
  ok: readResponseWithLimit rejected on oversized body тАФ true
  ok: bounded error message present (got: bedrock-mantle.model-discovery: JSON response exceeds 1048576 bytes) тАФ true
  ok: bytes on wire stayed near the cap, not the full body (sent 1064178) тАФ true

[negative control] OLD unbounded `await response.json()` buffers whole body
  ok: unbounded read pulled the FULL ~24 MiB body (sent 25165824), proving the cap is load-bearing тАФ true

[case 3] normal small catalog JSON still parses unchanged
  ok: small catalog body parsed into 1 model тАФ true
  ok: model id intact (valid small body not truncated) тАФ true

ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED
```

```text
Test Files  5 passed (5)
     Tests  48 passed (48)
  Duration  ~8s
```

**Label**: security

AI-assisted.
target.author: hugenshen
